title: Artificial Intelligence (AI) | Fragomen, Del Rey, Bernsen & Loewy LLP
content:
## Artificial Intelligence (AI)
Competition for talent is fierce for companies in the Artificial Intelligence (AI) industry. Individuals with AI talent may have employment options all over world, which means companies must secure highly skilled, globally mobile and often niche talent as soon as possible. This requires strategic thinking and rapid response times from your immigration provider.
We understand the needs of clients in the AI sector. We support AI companies ranging from start-ups to established industry players, as well as other companies that are enhancing their AI capabilities or expanding into new regions.
For those starting, scaling or growing in the AI industry, speed is critical. Our firm matches the speed of your business, providing proactive and responsive support—whenever and wherever you need it. With our extensive resources and global footprint, we can help you scale and stay compliant in an ever-changing global landscape.
Clients Value Us For:

### Our Proactive, Responsive Approach
We know speed is crucial. Our firm delivers efficient, high-caliber execution at scale, so you can move key talent where they need to be, in order to drive your organizational priorities forward.
We support fast-scaling AI and technology companies, building with you as you grow and helping with new market entry. We are agile and ready to support you where you need us—today, tomorrow and beyond, as you expand your footprint throughout the world.

### Workforce Planning and Strategic Advisory
We can help you develop a world-class immigration program, allowing you to attract top talent and maintain your competitive edge. With our robust immigration support, we take the burden off your Talent, HR, Global Mobility and Operations teams. Your goals become our shared goals.
We also collaborate seamlessly with your partners—including relocation providers, Employers of Record and corporate setup specialists—so you can expand with confidence.

### Government Strategies Expertise
With immigration frameworks evolving around the globe, it’s critical to remain updated on the latest changes, in order to ensure any potential hurdles to your talent mapping are overcome ahead of time.
We have our finger on the pulse of the newest requirements, as well as what may be on the horizon, allowing us to keep you compliant, agile and impactful. We can also advocate with you, so your voice is heard by those shaping policy around the globe, especially where top global talent is most in-demand in the AI space.
As a firm, we are highly dedicated to innovation, making us an ideal partner for AI-focused clients. We provide clients with our proprietary case management technology, as well as analytics and reporting.
Our approach streamlines the immigration experience and provides key insights for strategic decision-making.

## Explore more at Fragomen
Fragomen earned top national and regional rankings in Chambers USA 2026, including its 13th consecutive year in Band 1 nationwide.
Senior Manager Samantha Arnold examines the remaining opportunities under Ireland’s winding-down Immigrant Investor Programme and what they mean for eligible investors and their families.
Government Affairs Strategy Director Shuyeb Muquit analyses the UK’s latest net migration decline, exploring what the data reveals about policy impact, economic trade‑offs and the risks of over‑correction in future migration strategy.
Partner Bo Cooper discusses the practical considerations facing employers and foreign nationals amid recent changes to green card processing.
In this #FragomenFC episode, Partner Rick Lamanna, Senior Manager Sergio Flores and Senior Associate Jake Paul Minster discuss final travel and immigration considerations ahead of the 2026 FIFA World Cup.
Senior Manager Samantha Arnold discusses Ireland’s citizenship framework and the gaps that can remain following reforms to birthright citizenship.
In this video, Partner Diana Quintas discusses how Brazil’s family reunion visa supports long-term international assignments and outlines key eligibility, documentation and compliance considerations for employers and families relocating to Brazil.
Attorney María José Clarke explores Chile’s intensified immigration enforcement framework under President José Antonio Kast, examining new employer compliance obligations, work authorization rules for foreign nationals and the legal and financial consequences of unauthorized employment.
Senior Manager Jonathan Hill examines how the UK’s new RAG rating system and stricter compliance metrics could shape universities’ approaches to international student recruitment and sponsorship compliance.
Knowledge Management Director Ana Sofia Walsh and Senior Client Engagement Manager Soraya Driessen examine the European Commission’s EU Inc. proposal and its potential to reshape corporate structures across the EU, highlighting the resulting complexities and unresolved questions for immigration, work authorization and cross-border workforce mobility.
